content_en:
17. Du‘a on “The Night of Decree’

During this blessed night,’ when the angels descend down to earth with the angel Jibril, and when the earth is overwhelmed with peace and serenity until the break of dawn, and when the doors of Paradise are opened, the worshipper is encouraged to turn to Allah, and to ask of his needs for this world and the Hereafter.

247 Reported by al-Bukhari (# 1899), Muslim (# 1079) and others.

248-The Night of Decree is one of the odd nights of the last ten nights of Ramadan.

The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months, The angels come down, and the Holy Spirit, in it, with the Permission of their Lord, with all decrees. Peace! (It lasts) Until the break of dawn.”

‘A’ishah narrated that she asked the Prophet (3%): “O Messenger of Allah! What dua should I make on Laylat al-Qadr (the Night of Decree)?” He (#%) answered:

“Say, ‘O Allah! You are Forgiving, and love to forgive, so forgive mel”?

18. dua@ Inside the Ka‘bah

The Ka'bah is the holiest of all places, and the first mosque to be built for mankind. It is a sanctuary that has no comparison in the entire world. Therefore, it is no surprise that dua@ at this blessed place has greater chances of being answered.

Of course, the obvious question that everyone will ask in our times is: “But it is not possible to go inside the Ka‘bah except with great difficulty!” Know then that the 4zr (the semi-circular attachment that is opposite the Yemeni-corner and Black-stone wall) is actually a part of the Ka‘bah, and prayer inside the Air is exactly the same as praying inside the physical structure of the Ka‘bah. This because of the fact that the original Ka'bah built by Ibrahim was a rectangle, and its foundations included the area that is now the Ayr. The only reason that the Ar is not included in the present structure is because the Quraysh, when they re-built the Ka‘bah, ran out of materials to complete its original structure. Therefore, they were forced to leave out the portion that is known today as the Ayr. When

20 Narrated by al-Tirmidhi, Ibn Majah, and al-Hakim and authenticated in Sahih al-Jami’ # 4423.

*! Reported by Muslim (2/968) and others.

the Prophet (3%) re-conquered Makkah, he expressed his desire to ‘A’ishah that he wanted to rebuild the Ka'bah upon the original foundations of Ibrahim, but he feared that the new Muslims would not be able to handle such a dramatic change, so he left it on its present foundations.

Therefore, one should be eager to pray in the Ayr, and make du‘ a in it as well, for it is equivalent to praying in the actual Ka'bah.

19. Du‘a at Safa and Marwa

It is narrated in the authentic Sunnah that the Prophet (3%) would make long du' as at Safa and Marwa,’” so the Muslim who is eager

to follow the Sunnah of the Prophet (3%) should do likewise.

20. dua After Stoning the Jamarat

Likewise, it is narrated that the Prophet (#8) would stone the small Jamarah (one of the three pillars that is stoned in the last three days of Hajj), then face the qiblah, raise his hands, and make du’ a for a long time. He would then stone the middle Jamarah and do the same. When he stoned the large Jamarah, he would depart without making any du’ a.”

21. Du‘ on the Day of ‘Arafah

Although dua during the entire Hajj is a great act of worship, du‘ a on this particular day is of even greater importance. The Day of ‘Arafah is the essence and pinnacle of Hajj. On this great and momentous day, when millions of worshippers gather together at one plain, from every single corner of the globe, with only one purpose in mind — to respond to the call of their Creator — during this auspicious day, Allah does not refuse the requests of His worshippers.

a2 Reported by Muslim (# 1218) and others. 3 Reported by al-Bukhari (#1753) and others.

‘Amr ibn al-‘As narrated that the Prophet (#8) said:

“The best of all du‘ as is the one given on the Day of ‘Arafah, and the best statement that I or any of the prophets before me have said, is, ‘There is no deity worthy of worship, He is One,

and He has no Partners. To Him belongs the Dominion, to

Him is given all Praise, and He is aware of all things”.

22. The First Ten Days of Dhul-Hijjah The Prophet (3%) stated:

There are no days during which good deeds are more beloved to Allah than during these ten days.””

This hadith shows the general superiority of this time, and as du’ @ is one of the most beloved acts of worship, we are encouraged to make du‘ a during these ten days.

23. While Visiting the Sick

Umm Salamah narrated that the Prophet (3%) said:

“When you visit the sick, or the dead (body before its burial)

then say good, because the angels say, Amin to whatever you 99256

24. When the Soul of a Person is Taken

Umm Salamah narrated that the Prophet (#%) entered upon Abi

254Narrated by al-Tirmidhi, from ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr ibn al-'As, and authenticated by al-Albani in Sahih al-Jami’ # 3274.

255 Reported by al-Bukhari (# 969) and others. 56 Reported by Muslim (# 2126) and others.

Salamah (while he was on his death bed), and his eyes had fixed into a stare (i.e. he had died), so the Prophet (#%) closed his eyelids, and said:

“When the soul leaves the body, the eyes follow it”.

At this, the people of Abt. Salamah’s house began crying, and the Prophet (#8) further said:

“Do not ask for yourselves anything but good, for the angels

will say Amin to all that you ask for. O Allah, forgive Aba

Salamah, and raise his ranks among those who are guided...”

Therefore, whenever a person is in the presence of one who is about to die, he should pray to Allah for all that is good, for the ' dying person, his family, and for himself.

This hadith also explains why the eyes of those who are dead are always found to be in a stare, looking upwards, since the eyes follow the soul as it leaves upwards in the hands of the angels of death.

25. When Rain Falls

This is a time when the blessings of Allah descend from the Heavens, and so it is also one of the times when the du’ @ of a worshipper is accepted.

Sahl ibn Sa‘d narrated that the Prophet (#8) said:

“Two are the du’ as that are never returned: the du’a@ made when the prayer is being called, and at the time of rainfall”.

Plus there is the hadith that has already been given, namely:

“Seek the response to your du’ das when the armies meet, and

the prayer is called, and when rain falls”?

7 Narrated by Muslim, Aba Dawad and Ahmad, as mentioned in Sahih al-

258 Narrated by Aba Dawud (# 3540) and al-Hakim (2/114), who considered it to be sahih, al-Dhahabi agrees with him, as did al-Albani in Sahih al-

259 Reported by al-Shafi'i in his a/-Umm (1/223); authenticated in al-Sahthah,

The Prophet (3%), would pray four rak’ahs after the sun had reached its zenith (but before Zuhr), and he said,

This is an hour during which the gates of the skies are opened, and I like that some of my good acts are raised up (at this

In this hadith is an indication that a dy’ @ at this time (after the sun reaches its zenith but before the time of Zuhr) has more chances of being responded to, as the gates of the Heavens are opened.

27. At the Crowing of a Rooster Aba Hurayrah narrated that the Prophet (3%) said:

“When you hear a rooster crowing, then ask Allah for His Bounties, for it has seen an angel, and when you hear a donkey braying, then seek refuge in Allah from Shayfan, for it has

261 seen a Shaytan.”

Qadi ‘Iyad said, in reference to this hadith:

“It is as if (the reason that one is asked to make du’ a@ when he hears the rooster crow) is that it is likely that the angel will say Amin to the person’s du'@, and ask for his forgiveness, and testify to his sincerity and humbleness. And when (he hears a donkey braying), he should seek refuge from Allah for the evil that the Shaytan can cause him, and the evil thoughts that he can whisper to him, so at this occasion the worshipper is told to turn to Allah for protection...”

260 Reported by Ahmad (3/411), al-Tirmidhi (# 478) and others. Al-Tirmidhi considered it hasan gharib, and Ahmad Shakir considered it sahih (2/343).

261 Narrated by al-Bukhari, Muslim and Ahmad, as mentioned in Sahih al-

CONDITIONS DURING WHICH Du’ 4 1s ANSWERED

After a discussion of the times during which the dw '‘@ is likely to be answered, it is relevant to discuss the conditions during which a - worshipper’s dua is likely to be responded to.

1. The Person who has been Wronged

Ifa person has been wronged by someone, Allah accepts the du’ a of that person against the person that wronged him. This ‘wrong’ could be that he was cheated of his rights, oppressed, persecuted, slandered, or any other form of wrong that a person may be inflicted with.

The Prophet (#%) mentioned in numerous ahadith that a Muslim should fear the du'@ of he who has been wronged, for he (3) said:

“Fear the du’ a@ of he who has been wronged, for verily it as-

cends to the skies faster than sparks (of light)”.””
content_id:
17. Du'a pada "Malam Kemuliaan"

Pada malam yang penuh berkah ini, ketika para malaikat turun ke bumi bersama malaikat Jibril, dan ketika bumi diliputi dengan kedamaian dan ketenangan hingga terbit fajar, dan ketika pintu-pintu Surga dibuka, seorang hamba dianjurkan untuk menghadapkan diri kepada Allah, dan memohon segala kebutuhannya untuk dunia dan Akhirat.

247 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (# 1899), Muslim (# 1079) dan lainnya.

248 - Malam Kemuliaan adalah salah satu malam ganjil dari sepuluh malam terakhir Ramadan.

Malam Kemuliaan lebih baik dari seribu bulan. Para malaikat dan Ruh turun padanya dengan izin Tuhan mereka, membawa segala urusan. Sejahteralah (malam itu) hingga terbit fajar."

'A'ishah meriwayatkan bahwa ia bertanya kepada Nabi (3%): "Wahai Rasulullah! Du'a apa yang harus aku panjatkan pada Laylat al-Qadr (Malam Kemuliaan)?" Beliau (#%) menjawab:

"Ucapkanlah, 'Ya Allah! Engkau Maha Pengampun, dan mencintai pengampunan, maka ampunilah aku!'"

18. Du'a di Dalam Ka'bah

Ka'bah adalah tempat paling suci dari semua tempat, dan masjid pertama yang dibangun untuk umat manusia. Ia adalah sebuah tempat suci yang tidak ada bandingannya di seluruh dunia. Oleh karena itu, tidak mengherankan bahwa du'a di tempat yang penuh berkah ini memiliki peluang lebih besar untuk dikabulkan.

Tentu saja, pertanyaan yang jelas yang akan ditanyakan semua orang pada zaman kita adalah: "Tetapi tidak mungkin masuk ke dalam Ka'bah kecuali dengan kesulitan yang besar!" Ketahuilah bahwa Hijr (lampiran berbentuk setengah lingkaran yang berhadapan dengan sudut Yaman dan dinding Hajar Aswad) sebenarnya merupakan bagian dari Ka'bah, dan salah di dalam Hijr sama persis dengan salah di dalam struktur fisik Ka'bah. Hal ini karena Ka'bah asli yang dibangun oleh Ibrahim berbentuk persegi panjang, dan fondasinya mencakup area yang kini menjadi Hijr. Satu-satunya alasan mengapa Hijr tidak termasuk dalam struktur saat ini adalah karena kaum Quraysh, ketika mereka membangun kembali Ka'bah, kehabisan bahan untuk menyelesaikan struktur aslinya. Oleh karena itu, mereka terpaksa meninggalkan bagian yang kini dikenal sebagai Hijr. Ketika

20 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi, Ibn Majah, dan al-Hakim dan diotentikasi dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 4423.

*! Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (2/968) dan lainnya.

Nabi (3%) menaklukkan kembali Makkah, beliau mengungkapkan keinginannya kepada 'A'ishah bahwa beliau ingin membangun kembali Ka'bah di atas fondasi asli Ibrahim, namun beliau khawatir para Muslim baru tidak akan mampu menerima perubahan yang begitu drastis, sehingga beliau membiarkannya di atas fondasi yang ada sekarang.

Oleh karena itu, seseorang hendaknya bersemangat untuk salah di Hijr, dan juga berdoa di dalamnya, karena itu setara dengan salah di dalam Ka'bah yang sebenarnya.

19. Du'a di Safa dan Marwa

Diriwayatkan dalam Sunnah yang sahih bahwa Nabi (3%) akan memanjatkan du'a yang panjang di Safa dan Marwa, sehingga seorang Muslim yang bersemangat

untuk mengikuti Sunnah Nabi (3%) hendaknya melakukan hal yang sama.

20. Du'a Setelah Melempar Jamarat

Demikian pula, diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (#8) melempar Jamarah kecil (salah satu dari tiga pilar yang dilempari pada tiga hari terakhir Haji), kemudian menghadap kiblah, mengangkat tangannya, dan berdoa untuk waktu yang lama. Kemudian beliau melempar Jamarah tengah dan melakukan hal yang sama. Ketika beliau melempar Jamarah besar, beliau pergi tanpa berdoa.

21. Du'a pada Hari 'Arafah

Meskipun du'a selama seluruh ibadah Haji merupakan amal ibadah yang agung, du'a pada hari ini memiliki kepentingan yang lebih besar. Hari 'Arafah adalah inti dan puncak dari Haji. Pada hari yang agung dan bersejarah ini, ketika jutaan jamaah berkumpul bersama di satu dataran, dari setiap sudut penjuru bumi, dengan satu tujuan di benak mereka — untuk memenuhi panggilan Pencipta mereka — pada hari yang penuh berkah ini, Allah tidak menolak permohonan hamba-hamba-Nya.

a2 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (# 1218) dan lainnya. 3 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (#1753) dan lainnya.

'Amr ibn al-'As meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (#8) bersabda:

"Sebaik-baik du'a adalah yang dipanjatkan pada Hari 'Arafah, dan sebaik-baik pernyataan yang aku atau salah satu nabi sebelumku ucapkan adalah, 'Tiada ilah yang berhak disembah, Dia Maha Esa,

dan Dia tidak memiliki sekutu. Milik-Nya lah segala kekuasaan,

kepada-Nya lah segala pujian diberikan, dan Dia mengetahui segala sesuatu'".

22. Sepuluh Hari Pertama Dzulhijjah Nabi (3%) bersabda:

"Tidak ada hari-hari di mana amal saleh lebih dicintai oleh Allah daripada sepuluh hari ini."

Hadith ini menunjukkan keistimewaan umum waktu ini, dan karena du'a adalah salah satu amal ibadah yang paling dicintai, kita dianjurkan untuk berdoa selama sepuluh hari ini.

23. Ketika Menjenguk Orang Sakit

Umm Salamah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (3%) bersabda:

"Ketika kamu menjenguk orang sakit, atau (jenazah) sebelum dikuburkan,

maka ucapkanlah kebaikan, karena para malaikat mengucapkan Amin atas apa pun yang kamu ucapkan."

24. Ketika Ruh Seseorang Dicabut

Umm Salamah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (#%) masuk menemui Abu

254 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi, dari 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-'As, dan diotentikasi oleh al-Albani dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 3274.

255 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (# 969) dan lainnya. 56 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (# 2126) dan lainnya.

Salamah (saat beliau berada di ranjang kematiannya), dan matanya telah terpaku menatap (yaitu beliau telah meninggal), maka Nabi (#%) memejamkan kelopak matanya, dan bersabda:

"Ketika ruh meninggalkan tubuh, mata mengikutinya."

Mendengar itu, anggota keluarga Abu Salamah mulai menangis, dan Nabi (#8) melanjutkan:

"Janganlah kalian memohon untuk dirimu sendiri kecuali kebaikan, karena para malaikat akan mengucapkan Amin atas segala yang kamu mohon. Ya Allah, ampunilah Abu

Salamah, dan tinggikanlah derajatnya di antara orang-orang yang mendapat petunjuk..."

Oleh karena itu, setiap kali seseorang berada di dekat orang yang akan meninggal, ia hendaknya berdoa kepada Allah untuk segala kebaikan, bagi orang yang akan meninggal itu, keluarganya, dan bagi dirinya sendiri.

Hadith ini juga menjelaskan mengapa mata orang-orang yang telah meninggal selalu dalam keadaan terpaku menatap ke atas, karena mata mengikuti ruh saat ruh itu naik ke atas di tangan para malaikat maut.

25. Ketika Hujan Turun

Ini adalah saat ketika berkah Allah turun dari Langit, dan karenanya juga merupakan salah satu waktu ketika du'a seorang hamba dikabulkan.

Sahl ibn Sa'd meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (#8) bersabda:

"Ada dua du'a yang tidak pernah ditolak: du'a yang dipanjatkan ketika azan dikumandangkan, dan pada saat turunnya hujan."

Ditambah lagi hadith yang telah disebutkan sebelumnya, yaitu:

"Carilah pengabulan du'a kalian ketika pasukan bertemu, dan

ketika azan dikumandangkan, dan ketika hujan turun."

7 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim, Abu Dawud dan Ahmad, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Sahih al-

258 Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud (# 3540) dan al-Hakim (2/114), yang menganggapnya sahih, al-Dzahabi setuju dengannya, sebagaimana al-Albani dalam Sahih al-

259 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Syafi'i dalam al-Umm-nya (1/223); diotentikasi dalam al-Shahihah,

Nabi (3%) biasa salah empat raka'at setelah matahari mencapai zenith-nya (tetapi sebelum waktu Zuhur), dan beliau bersabda,

"Ini adalah waktu di mana pintu-pintu langit dibuka, dan aku suka beberapa amal baikku diangkat (pada waktu

Dalam hadith ini terdapat indikasi bahwa du'a pada waktu ini (setelah matahari mencapai zenith tetapi sebelum waktu Zuhur) memiliki peluang lebih besar untuk dikabulkan, karena pintu-pintu Langit terbuka.

27. Ketika Ayam Jantan Berkokok Abu Hurayrah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (3%) bersabda:

"Ketika kamu mendengar ayam jantan berkokok, maka mintalah kepada Allah karunia-Nya, karena ia telah melihat seorang malaikat, dan ketika kamu mendengar keledai meringkik, maka mohonlah perlindungan kepada Allah dari Syaitan, karena ia telah

261 melihat seorang Syaitan."

Qadi 'Iyad berkata, merujuk pada hadith ini:

"Seolah-olah (alasan seseorang dianjurkan untuk berdoa ketika mendengar ayam jantan berkokok) adalah bahwa kemungkinan besar malaikat akan mengucapkan Amin atas du'a orang tersebut, dan memohon ampunan untuknya, serta bersaksi atas ketulusan dan kerendahan hatinya. Dan ketika (mendengar keledai meringkik), ia hendaknya memohon perlindungan kepada Allah dari keburukan yang dapat ditimbulkan Syaitan kepadanya, dan pikiran-pikiran buruk yang dapat dibisikkannya, sehingga pada kesempatan ini seorang hamba diperintahkan untuk berpaling kepada Allah untuk memperoleh perlindungan..."

260 Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad (3/411), al-Tirmidhi (# 478) dan lainnya. Al-Tirmidhi menganggapnya hasan gharib, dan Ahmad Syakir menganggapnya sahih (2/343).

261 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari, Muslim dan Ahmad, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Sahih al-

KONDISI-KONDISI DI MANA Du'a DIKABULKAN

Setelah pembahasan mengenai waktu-waktu di mana du'a kemungkinan besar dikabulkan, relevan untuk membahas kondisi-kondisi di mana du'a seorang hamba kemungkinan besar akan direspons.

1. Orang yang Dizalimi

Jika seseorang dizalimi oleh orang lain, Allah menerima du'a orang tersebut terhadap orang yang menzaliminya. 'Kezaliman' ini bisa berupa dirampasnya hak-haknya, penindasan, penganiayaan, fitnah, atau bentuk kezaliman lainnya yang mungkin menimpa seseorang.

Nabi (ﷺ) menyebutkan dalam berbagai ahadith bahwa seorang Muslim hendaknya takut terhadap du'a orang yang dizalimi, karena beliau (ﷺ) bersabda:

"Takutlah terhadap du'a orang yang dizalimi, karena sesungguhnya ia naik ke langit lebih cepat dari percikan api (cahaya)"."
content_ar: 17. Du‘a on “The Night of Decree’ خلال this blessed night,’ عندما the الملائكة descend down to earth with the angel جبريل ﷺ, and عندما the earth is overwhelmed with peace and serenity حتى the break of dawn, and عندما the doors of الجنة are opened, the worshipper is encouraged to turn to الله, and to ask of his needs for this الدنيا and الآخرة. 247 Reported by البخاري (# 1899), مسلم (# 1079) and others. 248-The Night of Decree is one of the odd nights of the last ten nights of Ramadan. The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months, The الملائكة come down, and the Holy الروح, in it, with the Permission of their Lord, with all decrees. Peace! (It lasts) حتى the break of dawn.” ‘A’ishah narrated that she asked النبي ﷺ (3%): “O Messenger of الله! What الدعاء should I make on Laylat al-Qadr (the Night of Decree)?” He (#%) answered: “Say, ‘O الله! You are Forgiving, and المحبة to forgive, لذا forgive mel”? 18. الدعاء@ Inside the Ka‘bah The الكعبة المشرفة is the holiest of all places, and the أولاً المسجد to be built for mankind. It is a sanctuary that has no comparison in the entire الدنيا. لذلك, it is no surprise that الدعاء@ at this blessed place has greater chances of being answered. Of course, the obvious question that everyone will ask in our times is: “But it is not possible to go inside the Ka‘bah except with great difficulty!” Know then that the 4zr (the semi-circular attachment that is opposite the Yemeni-corner and Black-stone wall) is actually a part of the Ka‘bah, and الصلاة inside the Air is exactly the same as praying inside the physical structure of the Ka‘bah. This لأن of the fact that the original الكعبة المشرفة built by إبراهيم ﷺ was a rectangle, and its foundations included the area that is now the Ayr. The only العقل that the Ar is not included in the present structure is لأن the Quraysh, عندما they re-built the Ka‘bah, ran out of materials to complete its original structure. لذلك, they were forced to leave out the portion that is known today as the Ayr. عندما 20 Narrated by al-الترمذي, ابن ماجه, and al-Hakim and authenticated in صحيح al-Jami’ # 4423. *! Reported by مسلم (2/968) and others. النبي ﷺ (3%) re-conquered مكة المكرمة, he expressed his desire to ‘A’ishah that he wanted to rebuild the الكعبة المشرفة upon the original foundations of إبراهيم ﷺ, but he feared that the new المسلمون would not be able to handle such a dramatic change, لذا he left it on its present foundations. لذلك, ينبغي للمرء be eager to pray in the Ayr, and make du‘ a in it as well, for it is equivalent to praying in the actual الكعبة المشرفة. 19. Du‘a at Safa and Marwa روي in the authentic السنة that النبي ﷺ (3%) would make long du' as at Safa and Marwa,’” لذا the مسلم who is eager للاتباع the السنة of النبي ﷺ (3%) should do likewise. 20. الدعاء بعد Stoning the Jamarat Likewise, روي that النبي ﷺ (#8) would stone the small Jamarah (one of the three pillars that is stoned in the last three days of الحج), then face the القبلة, raise his hands, and make du’ a for a long time. He would then stone the middle Jamarah and do the same. عندما he stoned the large Jamarah, he would depart without making any du’ a.” 21. Du‘ on the Day of ‘Arafah وإن كان الدعاء خلال the entire الحج is a great act of worship, du‘ a on this particular day is of even greater importance. The Day of ‘Arafah is the essence and pinnacle of الحج. On this great and momentous day, عندما millions of worshippers gather together at one plain, from every single corner of the globe, with only one purpose in العقل — to respond to the call of their Creator — خلال this auspicious day, الله does not refuse the requests of His worshippers. a2 Reported by مسلم (# 1218) and others. 3 Reported by البخاري (#1753) and others. ‘Amr ibn al-‘As narrated that النبي ﷺ (#8) said: “The best of all du‘ as is the one given on the Day of ‘Arafah, and the best statement that I or any of the الأنبياء قبل me have said, is, ‘There is no deity worthy of worship, He is One, and He has no Partners. To Him belongs the Dominion, to Him is given all Praise, and He is aware of all things”. 22. The أولاً Ten Days of Dhul-Hijjah النبي ﷺ (3%) stated: There are no days خلال which الخير deeds are more beloved to الله than خلال these ten days.”” This الحديث shows the general superiority of this time, and as du’ @ is one of the most beloved acts of worship, we are encouraged to make du‘ a خلال these ten days. 23. بينما Visiting the Sick Umm Salamah narrated that النبي ﷺ (3%) said: “عندما you visit the sick, or the dead (الجسد قبل its burial) then say الخير, لأن the الملائكة say, آمين to whatever you 99256 24. عندما the النفس of a Person is Taken Umm Salamah narrated that النبي ﷺ (#%) entered upon Abi 254Narrated by al-الترمذي, from ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr ibn al-'As, and authenticated by al-Albani in صحيح al-Jami’ # 3274. 255 Reported by البخاري (# 969) and others. 56 Reported by مسلم (# 2126) and others. Salamah (بينما he was on his الموت bed), and his eyes had fixed into a stare (i.e. he had died), لذا النبي ﷺ (#%) closed his eyelids, and said: “عندما the النفس leaves the الجسد, the eyes follow it”. At this, the people of Abt. Salamah’s house began crying, and النبي ﷺ (#8) further said: “Do not ask for yourselves anything but الخير, for the الملائكة will say آمين to all that you ask for. O الله, forgive Aba Salamah, and raise his ranks among those who are guided...” لذلك, كلما a person is in the presence of one who is about to die, he should pray to الله for all that is الخير, for the ' dying person, his الأسرة, and for himself. This الحديث أيضاً explains why the eyes of those who are dead are always found to be in a stare, looking upwards, بما أن the eyes follow the النفس as it leaves upwards in the hands of the الملائكة of الموت. 25. عندما Rain Falls This is a time عندما the البركات of الله descend from the Heavens, and لذا it is أيضاً one of the times عندما the du’ @ of a worshipper is accepted. Sahl ibn Sa‘d narrated that النبي ﷺ (#8) said: “Two are the du’ as that are never returned: the du’a@ made عندما the الصلاة is being called, and at the time of rainfall”. Plus there is the الحديث that has already been given, أي: “Seek the response to your du’ das عندما the armies meet, and the الصلاة is called, and عندما rain falls”? 7 Narrated by مسلم, Aba Dawad and أحمد, as mentioned in صحيح al- 258 Narrated by Aba داود ﷺ (# 3540) and al-Hakim (2/114), who considered it to be صحيح, al-Dhahabi agrees with him, as did al-Albani in صحيح al- 259 Reported by al-الشافعي in his a/-Umm (1/223); authenticated in al-Sahthah, النبي ﷺ (3%), would pray four rak’ahs بعد the sun had reached its zenith (but قبل Zuhr), and قال, This is an hour خلال which the gates of the skies are opened, and I like that some of my الخير acts are raised up (at this In this الحديث is an indication that a dy’ @ at this time (بعد the sun reaches its zenith but قبل the time of Zuhr) has more chances of being responded to, as the gates of the Heavens are opened. 27. At the Crowing of a Rooster Aba Hurayrah narrated that النبي ﷺ (3%) said: “عندما you hear a rooster crowing, then ask الله for His Bounties, for it has seen an angel, and عندما you hear a donkey braying, then seek refuge in الله from Shayfan, for it has 261 seen a الشيطان.” Qadi ‘Iyad said, in reference to this الحديث: “It is as إذا (the العقل that one is asked to make du’ a@ عندما he hears the rooster crow) is that it is likely that the angel will say آمين to the person’s du'@, and ask for his المغفرة, and testify to his sincerity and humbleness. And عندما (he hears a donkey braying), he should seek refuge from الله for the الشر that the الشيطان can cause him, and the الشر thoughts that he can whisper to him, لذا at this occasion the worshipper is told to turn to الله for protection...” 260 Reported by أحمد (3/411), al-الترمذي (# 478) and others. Al-الترمذي considered it حسن gharib, and أحمد Shakir considered it صحيح (2/343). 261 Narrated by البخاري, مسلم and أحمد, as mentioned in صحيح al- CONDITIONS خلال WHICH Du’ 4 1s ANSWERED بعد a discussion of the times خلال which the dw '‘@ is likely to be answered, it is relevant to discuss the conditions خلال which a - worshipper’s الدعاء is likely to be responded to. 1. The Person who has been Wronged Ifa person has been wronged by someone, الله accepts the du’ a of that person against the person that wronged him. This ‘الخطأ’ could be that he was cheated of his الحقوق, oppressed, persecuted, slandered, or any other form of الخطأ that a person may be inflicted with. النبي ﷺ (#%) mentioned in numerous الأحاديث that a مسلم should الخوف the du'@ of he who has been wronged, for he (3) said: “الخوف the du’ a@ of he who has been wronged, for verily it as- cends to the skies faster than sparks (of light)”.””
